records.ety: [Pref. dis- + countenance: cf. OF. descontenancer, F. dcontenancer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Discountenanced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Discountenancing (?).]

defs: To ruffle or discompose the countenance of; to put of countenance; to put to shame; to abash.
quotes: How would one look from his majestic brow . . . Discountenance her despised!
The hermit was somewhat discountenanced by this observation.

defs: To refuse to countenance, or give the support of one's approval to; to give one's influence against; to restrain by cold treatment; to discourage.
quotes: A town meeting was convened to discountenance riot.

defs: Unfavorable aspect; unfriendly regard; cold treatment; disapprobation; whatever tends to check or discourage.
quotes: He thought a little discountenance on those persons would suppress that spirit.
